DMG: 34 Dmg Bonus: 165
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.

SV COLD: +15
WT: 20.0 Size: TINY
Class: WAR PAL SHD BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Post Comment
What mob drops this?
QuoteReply
# Apr 26 2005 at 3:56 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591796017332
11 posts
Found in Kael Drakkel, what mob drops this?
▲ Page top
RE: What mob drops this?
QuoteReply
# May 27 2005 at 1:13 PM Rating: Decent
Treesong
View User Forum
Scholar
169 posts
This is droped by Vealok the Angry: http://eqbeastiary.allakhazam.com/search.shtml?id=5675

I just looted one yesterday. I think it is his rare though, since I looted the chain sleeves twice before that. You can find him when you enter the Arenabuilding (where you will be face to face with three protectors of Zek, then go left, and immediately go left down the stairs again. You will find him in the short hallway there that leads to the actual arena, together with a regular giant, that did not add by the way when I pulled with my 66 BL. Not really harder then any other giant in that area. Don't think he even enraged, not sure though.
▲ Page top
magical?
QuoteReply
# Jan 31 2005 at 2:21 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592757817435
Scholar
19 posts
I have played EQ for 3 years, four next month. I feel really ignorant asking this, but what does MAGICAL do? You hit special mobs with it, or what? Anyway, I bought it today for 300 pp in the bazar to twink my new Ber and LOVING it. 75HP? Loving it
▲ Page top
RE: magical?
QuoteReply
# May 27 2005 at 1:22 PM Rating: Decent
Treesong
View User Forum
Scholar
169 posts
Magical indeed means that you can hit most every mob with it, unlike regular weapons that for instance can not hit willowisps, or Ghouls and several other (often undead) creatures. In the old days, this was a true issue for players, since WIllowisps offered good money and xp and so did several undead. So you wanted to get your hands on a Magical weapon asap so you could get rid of that vendorbought weapon, Cracked Staff, Gian Snake Fang or Minotaur Axe which were all non-magical. :) The only Magical weapons in those times that you could *buy* from a vendor were the "combine" weapons sold by the gypsies in North Karana and such. However, in those days having to pay 60 PP for a weapon was an insane amount. My first Magical weapon was a Runed Totem Staff, that I bought for 20 pp. :D

Nowadays, pretty much every looted weapon is Magical, though there are a few exeptions.

sweet blade
QuoteReply
# Nov 30 2002 at 3:04 PM Rating: Decent

I got this for around 500p as part of a twink set of equipment for an sk I was starting, right off the bat was doing max damage almost every hit, took on multiple reds for the first three levels no problem.
▲ Page top
Sweet weapon
QuoteReply
# Sep 12 2002 at 11:37 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591756055432
345 posts
I heard a shout last night, and checked out the lil link to this weapon...and just out of curiosity I asked how much. When he said 1.5k I was like sweeet. Major upgrade from the lame Argent Defender...and cost me less than that blade too. With 144 str and 100+ stamina I was hitting for around 160 maxed on mobs (around 90 max in pvp vs. a tough warrior) but sweet weapon indeed...added another 30pts of damage to my attacks not to mention a lil resist and hps too :P.

Renmazuo Skoaldiak of TZ

QuoteReply

▲ Page top
traded my BoX
QuoteReply
# Jun 15 2002 at 8:04 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591749847760
29 posts
I traded my BoX and 800pp for this. The BoX hit for 70 max Dmg. This hits for 100 max Dmg. On solo blue mobs, they go down faster with the Frost that with the BoX. Im a 32 Pally, 142 str, 101 sta, 78 agi, no haste (yet). I am very happy with my trade.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Apr 16 2002 at 2:01 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) well that weight is sure to waste your STA faster than a cheap hooker pumped up on speed
RE: heh
QuoteReply
# Jun 11 2002 at 3:18 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592253387254
2 posts
Well I am an sk/iskar was advertising for a better blde then my mith 2hs and I got a tell back saying he will sell me a sword for 1,200 that is better I asked if I could use it for class/race he said yes told me the st6ats I bought it for 1,200pp. He asked me to keep him informed what kind of damage this weapon does. I am a 42 and went to dl, joined a group I wear swiftclaw sash with 16% haste and an enchanter hasted me also. This weapon rocks I was hitting for 114 to 132 and I double for 120 each. then the str spells came and I was hitting for 132 to 160 and doubling for 140. this weapon is very sweet. And In my 50's as my str grows I prob will keep this weapon. Ty for selling it to me.
▲ Page top
STA doesn't matter much
QuoteReply
# Jun 04 2002 at 12:59 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591758106980
222 posts
Most folks that'll be using this thing are going to be troll and ogre SKs and maybe some warriors. When your STA is either over 100 or over 130 (forget which) then it doesn't matter when you kill your fatigue bar - you can't jump anymore, but your combat skills aren't effected at all.

I'm currently using this sucker on a 38 troll SK with silver chittin hand wraps - thanks to the haste, I'm swinging this sucker like there's no tommarrow - it's not uncommon for me to burn my fatigue bar. However, from logging a few fights were I got fatigued, I did confirm that my combat stats were not effected at my current 150ish STA - neither my speed nor my average damage was affected.

Plus side is that at my level, I can do up to 120+ on a single hit with this thing and usually average about 70 points a hit, which means that my double attacks can go for as much as 250 but are usually around 140 total damage - very nice indeed.
▲ Page top
RE: STA doesn't matter much
QuoteReply
# Jun 19 2002 at 2:04 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592251675539
38 posts
The STA minnimum is 100 for it not to effect str, agi, ect..

2 of them dropped at a guild raid tonight. I passed on the first one cause it wasn't as good a ratio than my weapon, but when the 2nd one dropped i said what the heck. I tested it out a bit and hit for 100 average. Will be nice to have a big dmg weapon for some big crits on raids.
higer delay=higher damage bonus. plus the hp are nice.I might make it a new resist item.

http://www.magelo.com/eq_view_profile.html?num=198360

RFB
QuoteReply
# Feb 22 2002 at 6:45 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591752130669
10 posts
A pet weapon? Morons, yes there are 1hs weapons, but if your a pally, you want 2hs eventually and that's that. I traded a Centi LS for this and I'm totally happy. At level 36 I hit for an average of 70 pts mind you that's an average and not the max I hit for. Now that it is magical, most of your complaints are useless, definately a good weapon. P.S. dam bonus is 11
▲ Page top
vs BABS
QuoteReply
# Feb 12 2002 at 3:02 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591758106980
222 posts
Was using a BABS when I picked this thing up - wasn't sure at first how ratios would compare but it's definately better in the long run -

BABS
Damage: 24
Delay: 39
Ratio: 1.625 Amazing
Poison Resist: +3
Weight: 2.5

RFB
Damage: 34
Delay: 49
Ratio: 1.441 Amazing
Cold Resist: +15 HP: +75
Weight: 20.0

Good thing I'm a troll with a good STR and STA - that wieght sucks.
▲ Page top
Had it for 15 Levs now
QuoteReply
# Jan 06 2002 at 9:11 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592250744841
9 posts
I picked this up for 200pp at level 24 for my SHD and I will not be selling it for quite some time. With Silver Hand Wraps or my FBSS it deals VERY good damage at at least what I feel is normal speed. When I evetualy equiped it on my 53 Ogre War I rarely hit for less than 100dam and get crits so far of over 270damn! Without a haste effect you still get good performance. The damage it deals realy SEEMS to make up for the 49dly, can't say it does or doesn't. Definately suited better to the stronger races and with it's wieght your stamina should be high or you will see it drop to zilch in one or two swings. I love it.

Yes it is magical now
QuoteReply
# Sep 06 2001 at 12:34 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591759882578
10 posts
I bought this sword today and found that it is MAGICAL now.

Many people say great delay = suck weapon, yep somehow it is. But for this 2hs a 34dmg is very good for making damage, even for a warrior. Remember that not ALL warriors are the main tank, sometimes we have to deal with damage and avoid taunt also, so I think this weapon still has its value.

Just my 2cp.


Hamk on Druzzil Ro
▲ Page top
now heres a steal of a deal
QuoteReply
# Aug 04 2001 at 7:01 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591763564101
49 posts
i have a lvl 37 sk on bristlebane back at lvl 22 i traded a silvery 2handed axe for it at the time i was only using my 2hs i had the axe and a nathsar greatsword....after testing this thing out my nathsar got banked for a very long time im now 15 lvls higher and everyone offers me things for this sword my 2hs is at 186 and my max hit thus far is 128....with 810 attack and 129 str =)
plus i kill for some serious double attacks not a bad pick up considering i traded a 100pp axe to get it...im just afraid to think what a twinked out ogre warrior would do with this thing =)
▲ Page top
Stealth upgrades...
QuoteReply
# Jul 21 2001 at 10:24 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249887789
4 posts
I traded for this back when it was 29/49 for a couple minor items under 1KPP.

3 patches ago I was quite happy with the reverse nerf to 34/49

Last patch I nearly fell over with the addition of magical status.
On my lvl 35 Warrior with 163 2HS and 128STR this thing out damages my old standby Jade Mace/ Stormfeather tallon combo. And absolutely no problem maintaining aggro. Taunt plus regular hits over 100 **** mobs off something fierce.

Best 1K ever spent.
